whatsHappening.background: 1948: The Nakba displaces about 750,000 Palestinians. 1967: Israel occupies Gaza, the West Bank, and East Jerusalem. 1987: the First Intifada begins. 1993: Oslo creates the Palestinian Authority and mutual recognition between the PLO and Israel.

parties.name: Israel
parties.description: Occupying power since 1967; conducting the current military campaign in Gaza.

parties.name: Hamas
parties.description: Armed group governing Gaza; launched the October 7 attack.

parties.name: Palestinian Authority / Fatah
parties.description: Governs parts of the West Bank; sidelined in Gaza.

parties.name: United States
parties.description: Israel's primary ally and arms supplier; brokered the ceasefire.

timeline.description: The Nakba: Israel declares independence; mass Palestinian expulsion begins.
Occupation begins after the Six Day War.

civilianImpact.experiences: 2007: Gaza blockade begins after Hamas seizes control. 2008-2023: repeated Israeli military operations kill thousands of Palestinians. October 7, 2023: Hamas attacks Israel, killing about 1,200 Israelis; Israel launches a massive military campaign on Gaza.

image.alt: Palestinians moving through destruction in Gaza

howToHelp.summary: October 2025: a ceasefire is reached, but Israel retains control of 53% of Gaza. In 2026, the war enters its 900th+ day and conditions remain dire.

learnMore.text: This slide covers the historical path to the current crisis.
learnMore.label: Next Slide

whatsHappening.background: The humanitarian toll is catastrophic. Families have been displaced repeatedly, basic services have collapsed, and children face mass trauma, orphanhood, hunger, and injury.

parties.name: 171,830 injured
parties.description: Mass casualty levels continue to rise.

timeline.description: Children who lost one or both parents.

civilianImpact.experiences: Famine has reached IPC Phase 5, the most severe classification: catastrophe/famine. It means starvation, mass death, and total collapse of livelihoods. In August 2025, famine was confirmed in Gaza.

image.alt: Projected acute food insecurity and malnutrition map for Gaza

howToHelp.summary: Almost a third of the population, 641,000 people, were expected to face catastrophic conditions, while another 1.14 million were projected in IPC Phase 4. Acute malnutrition in children under five is expected through June 2026.

learnMore.text: IPC Phase 5 is the highest famine classification and signals starvation, destitution, and death.
learnMore.label: IPC

slides.title: Palestine
slides.subtitle: Law and How You Can Help

whatsHappening.background: The ICC issued arrest warrants for Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and former Defense Minister Yoav Gallant for crimes against humanity and war crimes, including starvation as a method of warfare, attacks on civilians, murder, and persecution.

parties.name: PCRF
parties.description: Medical and humanitarian support for children.

timeline.description: Arrest warrants issued for war crimes and crimes against humanity.
International Court of Justice proceedings continue.
Legal, humanitarian, and advocacy pressure remains urgent.

civilianImpact.experiences: Help can take three forms: donate to trusted humanitarian organizations, educate yourself using reliable reporting and historical resources, and advocate by contacting representatives and amplifying Palestinian voices.

image.alt: Palestinians moving through destruction in Gaza

howToHelp.summary: Donate: UNRWA, PCRF, HEAL Palestine, Doctors Without Borders, UN Crisis Relief, and the World Food Programme. Educate: decolonizepalestine.com, btselem.org, and 972mag.com. Advocate: contact your representatives and amplify Palestinian voices.

learnMore.text: Additional education sources: btselem.org and 972mag.com. Advocacy starts with contacting your representatives and sharing verified on-the-ground reporting.
learnMore.label: B'Tselem
